NEXT (queued for next session — 2 open tickets, ordered for efficiency):

**SLOT 1 — (turnstile-app-env-guard) Tier 1 — mechanical hotfix, ~15-25 min**
Same DefinePlugin trap as (fix/auth-bypass-app-env-guard) ef17d8f and the socket/auth.ts portion of 7787469, but in a third file that was missed during the prior fixes. Location: `apps/web/src/server/lib/turnstile.ts:46` currently uses `env.NODE_ENV === "production"` to gate between test-key acceptance (dev) and real Cloudflare API verification (prod). Webpack DefinePlugin inlines `process.env.NODE_ENV` as `"production"` at `next build` so any containerized dev build will try to call Cloudflare's real `siteverify` endpoint with whatever the user submitted, even for the bypass-skipped path or the test Turnstile keys (`1x00000000000000000000AA`). Not blocking the current smoke (bypass entirely skips Turnstile) but blocks the real credentials login flow in containerized dev. Fix: switch `env.NODE_ENV` → `env.APP_ENV` per the pattern locked in [[webpack-define-plugin-trap]]. TDD: extract the prod-gate as a pure helper (similar to `isE2EBypassEnabled` shape) with 2-3 RED→GREEN cases covering dev/staging/prod. After implementation: rebuild app container, verify the bundle no longer hardcodes "production" inlining in the turnstile path (grep test). Document as a 🟡 fix (not a new gotcha — same trap as [[webpack-define-plugin-trap]] which is the framework-level rule). Branch: `fix/turnstile-app-env-guard`. Same squash-merge flow.

**SLOT 2 — (incoming-call-dialog-not-rendered) Tier 1-2 — investigation, ~45-90 min**
After the 2026-05-23 PM smoke, alice clicking "Call Sales (online)" successfully creates a call (URL changes to /app/call/[id]) and opens the LiveKit signal connection. But bob's page (also at /app, signed in via bypass as bob@yelli.local, Sales dept binding) shows `[role="dialog"]` count = 0 — no IncomingCallDialog renders. The actual cause is one of: (a) IncomingCallDialog is not mounted on the /app route at all (only on some other layout subtree); (b) the socket emits `call:incoming` but bob's client-side listener isn't subscribed yet because of a fresh-client race (similar to but distinct from the resolved [[fresh-client-presence-snapshot-race]]); (c) the server-side `call:incoming` payload doesn't reach bob because of tenant/org/dept filtering in the socket server's emit logic. Investigation steps for next session: (1) grep `IncomingCallDialog` to find where it's mounted in the component tree and confirm /app contains it; (2) Playwright instrument bob's page to capture all `framereceived` events on the Socket.IO WebSocket — verify whether `call:incoming` ever arrives at bob's client; (3) if it doesn't arrive: trace the server-side emit path in `apps/web/src/server/socket/server.ts` (or equivalent) for any filter that excludes bob; (4) if it arrives but dialog doesn't render: trace the React handler that's supposed to set dialog state. Use the same multi-context rig technique from [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-22]]. This is the LAST blocker before the smoke can be converted from PARTIAL → FULL per [[phase-7-realtime-engine-closeout-criterion]].

After both: re-run the full multi-context smoke. If both #14 (yellow-dot indicator confirmation) and #15 (IncomingCallDialog) pass, the smoke is FULL and Phase 7 realtime engines are closed at the strongest possible gate. Otherwise file a third follow-up.
